title: Autonomous Garbage Collector
desc: An autonomous robot that navigates using computer-vision-based obstacle detection and collects trash along the streets and communial areas
overview: The AGC is a robotics project aimed at addressing the growing litter problem in South African communities through autonomous technology.
The project proposes a self-driving, robotic vehicle capable of detecting and collecting garbage in public spaces without human intervention.
The vehicle features a 6-wheel omni-directional drive system for navigating varied terrain, a robotic arm for picking up litter, an onboard trash
compartment, and a suite of sensors for object detection and environmental awareness. A companion drone is also planned to serve as an aerial scout
and target locator. Development is structured across three iterative variants — from a small proof-of-concept prototype (Star 1), to a scaled hardware
and software validation build (Star 2), through to a full-scale deployment-ready unit (Star 3). Each variant must meet defined criteria before the next
is initiated. The broader goal of the AGC project is not to fully solve the litter crisis, but to demonstrate and validate the use of autonomous technology
as a practical solution to real-world community problems.
challenge: One of the greatest challenges that I have faced thus far in developemnt of this projetc, is the detection model training and validation
Gathering a large enought data set proved to be time consuming as there aren't satisfactory online data sets to work from.
The final challenge being faced, is the object to arm co-ordination where aligning the orbotic hand with the detected object has to vary
by the trash eing collected and it's orientation.
built: See GitHub for demo object detection models and current STL designs for robotic arm
outcomes: The greatest outcome thus far, is the increased understanding of object detection technologies and software to hardware co-ordination.

title: Classical Tracker
desc: A simple application designed to help beginner pianist learn, track and stay motivated while they learn to read and inetrpret sheet music.
overview: Classical Tracker is a personal native application built to support my journey learning classical piano.
Designed purely for personal use, it serves as a centralised hub for managing sheet music, tracking practice progress, and storing recordings.
Key features include local sheet music storage with dedicated viewer window and auto-scroll playback, session recording for personal
practice takes, and the ability to store professional recordings for reference. Since the app is built for a single user, it skips unnecessary
complexity like accounts and authentication, keeping everything straigthforward and locally stored — with optional cloud backup.
The goal is a clean, focused tool that keeps everything a pianist needs in one place.
challenge: The project this far has shown no significnt hurdles other than the intergration between certain logics and expected UI/UX desired output.
built: View project on GitHub for application documentation and designs.

title: Trading Bot
desc: A simple trading Bot designed to trade the GBPUSD & EURUSD currency pairs, using machine learning to analyse patterns and make estimated trading decisions.
overview: This is an AI-powered swing trading bot build on MetaTrader 5, designed to trade the EURUSD and GBPUSD currency pairs.
It combines machine learning with multi-timeframe strategy, using higher timeframes(H4) to determine directional bias and
a lower timeframe(M15) for precise trade entries. The bot uses LightGBM gradieent boosting models and a regime detection
system to adapt to changing market conditions. The system features a fully deterministic risk managment engine that caps
exposure at both the trade and portfolio level, a news filter that pauses traidng during high-impact economic events,
and a quartely model refresh process to keep predictions relevant over time. All trade data is logged locally via SQLite.
The target performance was a 20-30% annual return with a maximum drawdown of 10%.
challenge: The greatest challenge faced was finding the correct parameter settings in the decision engine that would allow for a decently
staisfactory sortino ratio, however the more demanding the parameters became, the more occurences of the model Reward Hacking.
Therefor, stricter rules were placed in the rewards algorithm, however that resulted in low sortino ratios
built: Have a look on GitHub for the full Bot folder. NB: certain csv files are not supplied in repository!
outcomes: Gained a deeper understanding of Market Mechanics and also learned how to design efficient reward fucntions that produce

title: A-EYE Trainer for Dummies
desc: An application designed for beginner coders to aid them in training their own very first detection models while keeping UI simple and logic straightforward.
overview: AI Trainer for Dummies is a desktop application designed to make training custom object detection models accessible to anyone,
regardless of their machine learning background. The app provides everything needed to go from raw images to a trained model
in one place — no external tools, no complex setup, no prior ML knowledge required. Users can annotate their images with a
built-in bounding box tool, manage their datasets, configure basic training parameters, and monitor their model's training
progress in real time. Once training is complete, the model can be exported and used immediately. The only external requirement
is the user's own computing power. The goal is to remove the barrier of entry to machine learning by wrapping the complexity
of model training behind a clean and intuitive interface.
